nodes.title: Tokenize class names before matching
nodes.why: Splitting className avoids partial-text bugs and handles multiple classes safely.
nodes.tradeoff: Tiny parsing cost, but more reliable class checks.

nodes.title: Guard matcher inputs up front
nodes.why: Invalid nodes or selectors should return false instead of throwing.
nodes.tradeoff: One early check for more predictable behavior.

nodes.title: Handle #id and .class selectors with direct branches
nodes.why: These are common selectors, so direct branches keep logic simple.
nodes.alternative: Force every selector through one generic parser.
nodes.tradeoff: More branches, but easier to read and debug.

nodes.title: Compare tags case-insensitively
nodes.why: DOM-like tag casing can vary, so normalization prevents false negatives.
nodes.tradeoff: Extra lowercase conversion, but robust matching.

nodes.title: Return null early for invalid closest() inputs

anchor.snippet: if (!node || typeof selector !== 'string' || selector.trim() === '') return null;

nodes.why: closestSimple should fail softly and never throw on bad inputs.
nodes.tradeoff: Softer API behavior, but less strict error signaling.

nodes.title: Check self first, then walk ancestors
nodes.why: This mirrors native closest semantics: include the starting node if it matches.
nodes.alternative: Start from parentNode and skip self.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra match check, but correct expected behavior.
